title: The Problem with Anchors
concept: Anchor boxes require manual configuration, waste computational resources on background clutter, and struggle with irregular object shapes.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the primary role of default/anchor boxes in detectors like SSD?

options.text: They serve as preset shape stencils that the model adjusts to fit objects
They crop the image files to save disk storage
They classify the color of the background pixels

exercises.explain: Correct! Anchor boxes provide reference templates for bounding box predictions.

cards.title: The Anchor Headache
cards.body: For years, anchor boxes were the gold standard for object detection. But they are a developer's nightmare. If you change your dataset, you must manually redesign your anchors! Why do anchor boxes cause so many headaches?

cards.title: The Problem with Anchors
cards.body: Anchor-based models require developers to manually tune anchor hyperparameters (sizes, aspect ratios, and scales). They also create a massive class imbalance (99% of anchors are just empty background) and waste CPU power calculating overlaps for thousands of empty boxes.

cards.title: The Shoe Box Closet
cards.body: Imagine organizing your shoes by buying 100 plastic boxes of different sizes first. If you buy a new pair of tall boots, they might not fit any box! You have to throw them out or buy a new box shape. That's the anchor box problem.

cards.title: Drone Footages
cards.body: A developer trains an anchor-based model on street photos. When deploying the model on drone footage, it fails because birds-eye-view cars look like small squares, while the preset anchor templates are designed for long, horizontal cars seen from the side.

cards.title: More Anchors, Better Accuracy?
cards.body: You might think adding 20 anchors per grid cell increases accuracy. Fact: More anchors create massive negative box clutter and slow down the model, which can actually hurt both speed and classification accuracy.

cards.title: Anchor-Based vs. Anchor-Free Output
cards.body: How anchor templates compare to direct regression:

left.text:
Tans of preset box templates per cell
Requires manual shape tuning
High CPU waste evaluating empty boxes

right.text:
Zero preset templates
Direct pixel-to-border offset prediction
Simpler, cleaner design

exercises.prompt: Why is manual anchor box selection considered a drawback?

options.text: Anchors must be manually tuned for every specific dataset, which is tedious and error-prone
They make the loss function calculation impossible
They prevent the model from using GPU acceleration
They can only detect square objects

exercises.explain: Correct! Anchors are dataset-specific; changing the objects requires recalculating anchor sizes.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Anchor-based models create a large class imbalance because they generate thousands of negative (background) boxes for every positive object.
exercises.explain: True. The model spends a lot of energy learning that empty anchor boxes are background, which is inefficient.

exercises.sentence: Anchor boxes require manual tuning of sizes and ___ ratios to fit the objects in the dataset.
exercises.explain: Aspect ratios (width vs. height) are key hyperparameters for anchor box designs.

exercises.prompt: What happens when an anchor-based model encounters an object with an aspect ratio it has never seen?

options.text: It struggles to detect the object accurately because none of its stencils fit well
It crashes the model and stops the program
It automatically creates a new stencil on the fly

exercises.explain: Correct! Anchor-based models are limited by their preset templates.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a model to detect weirdly shaped microscopic cells that stretch and fold. Why should you avoid an anchor-based detector here?

options.text: The cells have highly variable and irregular aspect ratios that cannot be easily captured by a few rigid template box shapes.
Anchor-based models cannot run on microscopic images.
Microscopic cells do not have center points.
Anchor-based models are only designed for cars and dogs.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Irregular, shifting shapes are extremely difficult to model with fixed anchor templates.
